name: AWS EKS Karpenter Platform Baseline
description: Creates an EKS cluster baseline prepared for Karpenter autoscaling with bootstrap nodes, interruption handling, and controller IAM policy outputs.
architectureSummary: Builds an EKS control plane, a small managed bootstrap node group, core add-ons, a Karpenter controller IAM policy, an SQS interruption queue, EventBridge rules for EC2 interruption signals, and a generated NodePool/EC2NodeClass manifest.
securityNotes: Karpenter permissions are scoped to cluster-tagged resources where practical. The blueprint emits manifest YAML rather than applying Kubernetes resources directly, keeping cluster bootstrap reviewable in Git.

inputs.name: project_name
inputs.label: Project Name
inputs.description: Lowercase project name used for resource names.

inputs.name: subnet_ids
inputs.label: Private Subnet IDs
inputs.description: Comma-separated private subnet IDs for EKS and nodes.

inputs.name: bootstrap_instance_types
inputs.label: Bootstrap Instance Types
inputs.description: Comma-separated EC2 instance types for the small managed bootstrap node group.

inputs.name: karpenter_instance_families
inputs.label: Karpenter Instance Families
inputs.description: Comma-separated instance families allowed in the generated NodePool manifest.

inputs.name: karpenter_capacity_types
inputs.label: Karpenter Capacity Types
inputs.description: Comma-separated capacity types for the generated NodePool manifest.

outputs.name: interruption_queue_name
outputs.description: SQS queue name for Karpenter interruption handling.

outputs.name: karpenter_controller_policy_arn
outputs.description: IAM policy ARN for a Karpenter controller role.

outputs.name: karpenter_nodepool_manifest
outputs.description: Rendered Karpenter EC2NodeClass and NodePool manifest.
